extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The flaw is transition t34: on send_attempt, if (opted_out AND stage >= 10), it sets sent := true. This directly contradicts the property whenever the state (opted_out=true, stage>=10) is reachable. It is reachable: (1) opted_out is set true by t1 (optout) and is never reset to false by any transition; (2) all stage-advancing transitions guard only on stage, not on opted_out, so they fire freely while opted_out is true (each also sets sent:=false, but that does not matter). The unique key sequence that walks stage 0->10 is determined by the advancing transitions: stage0 key_1 (t3), stage1 key_1 (t6), stage2 key_2 (t9), stage3 key_1 (t12), stage4 key_1 (t15), stage5 key_0 (t18), stage6 key_0 (t21), stage7 key_1 (t24), stage8 key_1 (t27), stage9 key_1 (t30) -> stage 10. Counterexample execution (12 events, within the 13-event bound): optout sets opted_out=true; then the 10 keys [key_1,key_1,key_2,key_1,key_1,key_0,key_0,key_1,key_1,key_1] drive stage to 10; then send_attempt triggers t34 (opted_out=true, stage=10>=10), setting sent:=true. The resulting state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating G(opted_out implies not sent).
